task.title: Replacing the Drive Pulley with a Clamping Bush
task.shortdesc: In addition to installing the drive pulley with a feather key connection, it can also

context.note: In addition to installing the drive pulley with a feather key connection, it can also optionally be installed with a clamping bush.

step.cmd: With the cullet crusher empty:

ul.li: Stop the cullet crusher using the stop pushbutton at the control cabinet.
Switch off the lockable main switch at the control cabinet.
Lockout-tagout-verify the power. Follow plant procedures.
Verify that the feed unit is switched off and cannot be started up.

step.cmd: Remove the screw connections on the protective cover for the V-belts.

ul.li: Lift off the protective cover from the V-belts (1).

step.cmd: Loosen/remove the screw connection of the drive unit (2), and turn the tensioning screws (3) to relax the V-belts.
Remove the V-belts (4) from the cullet crusher’s drive pulley (5).
Remove the V-belt pulley and clamping bush.

ul.li: Remove the screw connection at the clamping bush (6) using an Allen key.
Lubricate the screws, and screw into the removal bore holes (7). (The number of screws depends on the size of the clamping bush.)
Screw the screws evenly into the removal bore holes until the clamping bush (8) has been pushed out of the hub of the V-belt pulley (9).
The V-belt pulley should move freely on the rotor shaft.
Pull the V-belt pulley and clamping bush from the rotor shaft (10).

step.cmd: Clean all smooth surfaces (e.g. bore holes, shaft, inside of clamping bush).

ul.li: Before performing cleaning work, seal all openings so that no water or cleaning agents are allowed to penetrate for operational or safety reasons (motors, cables, etc.).
Do not use any aggressive cleaning agents (pH value equal to or greater than 7).
Do not use any cleaning agents that contain chlorine.
After cleaning, apply a thin film of oil to bare metal surfaces.

step.cmd: Insert the clamping bush and the new V-belt pulley into one another.

ul.li: Align the holes in the clamping bush with the holes in the V-belt pulley.
Half-threaded holes must be opposite half smooth holes.

step.cmd: Lubricate the screws, and screw them in hand tight.
Place the feather key in the feather key groove of the rotor shaft (if present).
Lightly grease the shaft end of the rotor with lubricating grease or assembly paste.
Push the V-belt pulley with clamping bush into the rotor shaft.

ul.li: Use a torque wrench to tighten the screws evenly to the predefined tightening torque, as defined in the table in Section 4.

step.cmd: Fill open holes with lubricating grease.
Place the V-belts over the take-up pulley of the drive unit.
Align the take-up and drive pulleys straight with one another.
Tension the V-belts by turning at the tensioning screws.

ul.li: Verify that the take-up and drive pulleys are correctly aligned with one another.
Firmly tighten the screw fixing of the drive unit using a spanner or similar tool.

step.cmd: Mount the protective cover for the V-belts using screw connections.
Remove the lockout tagout.

ul.li: Switch on the cullet crusher using the lockable main switch.

step.cmd: Switch on the cullet crusher with the lockable main switch.

ul.li: Start the cullet crusher using the start pushbutton at the control cabinet.

step.cmd: After 30-60 minutes of operation, check the tightening torque of the clamping bush screws.

ul.li: If necessary, re-tighten as indicated in the table in Section 4.

step.cmd: Check the tension of the V-belts after approximately 10 to 15 hours of operation.

ul.li: Tension the V-belts until they can be pressed in or out by approximately 3 - 5 mm in the middle.
The V-belts must not slip when the cullet crusher is running. If they do, re-tension the V-belts.
When the V-belt tension has been adjusted, firmly tighten the fixing screws of the drive unit using a spanner or similar tool.
